name: 分数与小数
subject: math
curriculum_points:
SL 3.2
Content Guidance, clarification and syllabus links
Use of sine, cosine and tangent ratios to find the
sides and angles of right-angled triangles. In all areas of this topic, students should be
encouraged to sketch well-labelled diagrams to
support their solutions.
Link to: inverse functions (SL2.2) when finding
angles.
a b c This section does not include the ambiguous case of
the sine rule.
The sine rule: = = .
sinA sinB sinC
the sine rule.
The cosine rule: c2=a2+b2−2abcosC;
a2+b2−c2
cosC= .
2ab
Area of a triangle as absinC.

SL 3.3
Content Guidance, clarification and syllabus links
Applications of right and non-right angled trigonometry, including Pythagoras’s theorem.
Angles of elevation and depression.
Construction of labelled diagrams from written statements.
